zh.of Nantwich,Cheshire,England: 英國英格蘭爾南特維其
zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert le Maisterson於大約1284年出生在英國爾南特維其時，他的父親Robert Maisterson of Wich Malbank26歲，母親Cecilia Maisterson Mrs24歲。他於1312年之前與Agnes Wilbrugham在英國英格蘭爾南特維其結婚。他們至少是7個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.of Nantwich,Cheshire,England: 英国英格兰爾南特維其
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert le Maisterson于大约1284年出生在英国爾南特維其时，他的父亲Robert Maisterson of Wich Malbank26岁，母亲Cecilia Maisterson Mrs24岁。他于1312年之前与Agnes Wilbrugham在英国英格兰爾南特維其结婚。他们至少是7个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Maisterson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English maister ‘master’ + -son. Compare Master .
Irish: in Ireland, this is usually the English name in 1 above. However, it was also adopted for Irish Mac an Mháighistir ‘son of the master’, also Anglicized as McMaster .

variants.name: Master
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Amar, Dhimant, Pravin, Amit, Aruna, Bhanu, Gaurang, Gopal, Haren, Jagu, Jayesh, Kalpesh.

English: occupational name from Middle English maister ‘master’ (Old French maistre). The word had a wide range of applications, and the reference in surnames may be to a schoolmaster, to the master of a farm or house, to the master of an apprentice, or to a ship's captain. In Scotland the eldest sons of barons and the uncles of lords were called Masters, but this is not primarily a Scottish surname.
German: dialect form of   Meister   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: McMaster
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Mháighistir ‘son of the master’, meaning ‘teacher’ or ‘cleric’. It could also be applied in the sense of ‘lord’ or ‘owner’. This name was sometimes Anglicized as Masterson .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Lewis   or respelling of   Lou  , as borne by the impresario  Lew Grade  (  1906–98 , originally  Louis Winogradsky ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert le Maisterson was born about 1284, in Nantwich, Cheshire, England, his father, Robert Maisterson of Wich Malbank, was 26 and his mother, Cecilia Maisterson Mrs, was 24. He married Agnes Wilbrugham before 1312, in Nantwich, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert le Maisterson est né environ 1284, à Nantwich, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Robert Maisterson of Wich Malbank, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Cecilia Maisterson Mrs, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Agnes Wilbrugham avant 1312, à Nantwich, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert le Maisterson um 1284 in Nantwich, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert Maisterson of Wich Malbank 26 und seine Mutter Cecilia Maisterson Mrs 24. Er hat vor 1312 in Nantwich, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Agnes Wilbrugham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert le Maisterson è nato il circa 1284 nel Nantwich, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Robert Maisterson of Wich Malbank, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Cecilia Maisterson Mrs, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Agnes Wilbrugham prima del 1312, nel Nantwich, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert le Maisterson(이)가 대략 1284년 영국 체실르 난트위치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Maisterson of Wich Malbank(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Cecilia Maisterson Mrs(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1312년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 난트위치에서 Agnes Wilbrugham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들을 두었다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert le Maisterson родился около 1284 г. в местности нантвич, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert Maisterson of Wich Malbank было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Cecilia Maisterson Mrs, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Wilbrugham перед 1312 г. в местности нантвич, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей.

Se casó con Agnes Wilbrugham antes de 1312, en Nantwich, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos.
